title: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil

seo.title: Classic Hummus Recipe: Creamy, Easy & Authentic Dip
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best classic hummus recipe at home! This easy, creamy Middle Eastern dip with tahini & olive oil is perfect for any appetizer. Get the full recipe!
seo.h1: Classic Hummus Recipe: Creamy & Authentic Middle Eastern Dip
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic hummus recipe drizzled with olive oil, sprinkled with paprika and fresh parsley, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Whip up this incredibly creamy and flavorful classic hummus recipe at home with fresh ingredients!

description: This classic Middle Eastern hummus recipe delivers a velvety smooth texture and rich, nutty flavor that will transport your taste buds. Made with simple, fresh ingredients like chickpeas, tahini, lemon, and garlic, it is an essential dip for any mezze platter. Drizzle with good quality olive oil and serve with warm pita bread for an authentic culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: drained, rinsed, skins removed if desired for extra smoothness
good quality, stir well before measuring

instructions.title: Prepare Chickpeas and Aromatics
instructions.text: Drain and rinse the canned chickpeas thoroughly. For an extra smooth hummus, gently rub the chickpeas between your palms or with a kitchen towel to remove their skins; discard the skins. Peel and roughly chop the garlic cloves. Squeeze fresh lemon juice.

instructions.title: Create the Tahini Base
instructions.text: In a food processor, combine the tahini, fresh lemon juice, chopped garlic, and salt. Process for about 1-2 minutes until the mixture is lightened in color and thick. Scrape down the sides of the bowl.

instructions.title: Add Chickpeas and Blend
instructions.text: Add the prepared chickpeas to the tahini mixture in the food processor. If using, add the ground cumin. Process for 3-5 minutes, scraping down the sides occasionally, until the mixture starts to break down and become pasty.

instructions.title: Achieve Creamy Texture with Ice Water
instructions.text: With the food processor running, slowly drizzle in the ice water, one tablespoon at a time, until the hummus reaches your desired creamy and smooth consistency. This may take 5-10 minutes of continuous blending to achieve a truly velvety texture.

instructions.title: Adjust Seasoning and Chill
instructions.text: Taste the hummus and adjust salt or lemon juice as needed. Process for another minute to fully incorporate any adjustments. For best flavor and texture, transfer the hummus to a bowl, cover, and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Before serving, give the hummus a quick stir. Spoon it into a shallow serving bowl, creating a swirl on top with the back of a spoon. Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil, sprinkle with paprika, and garnish with fresh chopped parsley or cilantro. Serve with warm pita bread, fresh vegetables, or as part of a mezze platter.

tips: For the silkiest hummus, remove the skins from the chickpeas. It takes a little extra time but makes a noticeable difference in texture.
Always use a good quality tahini; it is the heart of the hummus flavor. If your tahini is too thick, whisk it with a little water before adding to the food processor.
Hummus tastes best at room temperature, allowing its flavors to fully develop. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.
article: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil is a beloved Middle Eastern Appetizers dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
